mnemonics: CIA Triad: Confidentiality, Integrity, Availability - The three pillars of information security.
AAA: Authentication, Authorization, Accounting - The three steps to control access to a system.
keyPoints: The primary goal of information security is to protect the confidentiality, integrity, and availability (CIA triad) of data.
Risk management involves identifying assets, threats, vulnerabilities, and implementing controls to mitigate risk.
Security controls are categorized as physical, technical, or administrative safeguards.
commonMistakes: Focusing only on technical security while neglecting physical and administrative (policy) controls.
Equating compliance (e.g., passing an audit) with being truly secure.
Underestimating the human element (social engineering) as a major threat vector.
